summary: No additional deterministic setup is needed. `org.springframework:spring-test:7.0.0` is an in-process test support library; its published runtime dependency is only `spring-core`, and the existing scaffold already adds the Spring context and test-framework dependencies needed by the current tests. No Docker-backed service or special library bootstrap is required.
agent_guidance: No special external setup is required. Keep coverage in-process against Spring TestContext/AOT APIs; note that Spring 7 registers `@WebAppConfiguration` resource base paths with recursive `**` resource patterns, so assertions copied from Spring 6 single-level `*` expectations may be version-sensitive.
risks: Spring Test exposes many optional integrations such as MockMvc, WebTestClient, JUnit, Mockito, and servlet APIs, but those are scenario-specific and should not be added unless the generated test intentionally exercises those integrations.
The reported failure appears to be a Spring 7 behavioral/API expectation change in the test, not a missing dependency or service setup problem.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8258 [Automation] java run fails for org.springframework:spring-test:7.0.0; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015
